name: PostgreSQL
description: Advanced open-source relational database known for standards compliance, extensibility, and rich feature set including JSONB, full-text search, window functions, CTEs, and support for complex queries.

name: MySQL
description: Widely-used open-source relational database, the default choice for LAMP stack applications. Offers high read performance, replication, and broad hosting support. InnoDB is the default ACID-compliant storage engine.

name: MariaDB
description: Community-developed fork of MySQL, offering drop-in compatibility with additional features like Aria storage engine, columnar storage via MariaDB ColumnStore, and more liberal open-source licensing.

name: SQLite
description: Serverless, file-based relational database engine embedded directly in applications. The most widely deployed database engine in the world, used in browsers, mobile apps, IoT devices, and desktop software.

name: Microsoft SQL Server
description: Enterprise relational database from Microsoft, tightly integrated with the Windows ecosystem and Azure cloud. Offers advanced features including Always On availability groups, columnstore indexes, and deep .NET and BI tooling integration.

name: Oracle Database
description: Enterprise-grade relational database system known for high reliability, scalability, and advanced features such as Real Application Clusters (RAC), Autonomous Database, and Exadata. Dominant in large enterprise and financial sector deployments.

name: MongoDB
description: Leading document-oriented database that stores data as flexible BSON documents. Provides a rich query language, horizontal scaling via sharding, and multi-document ACID transactions since version 4.0.

name: CouchDB
description: Apache document database designed for offline-first and sync scenarios. Uses JSON documents, MapReduce views, and an HTTP/REST API. Offers built-in multi-master replication with conflict detection.

name: Redis
description: In-memory data structure store used as a cache, message broker, and primary database. Supports rich data types (strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams) with optional persistence and Lua scripting.

name: Memcached
description: High-performance, distributed memory caching system designed for simplicity. Stores only string key-value pairs with no persistence, making it extremely fast for pure caching workloads. Often used alongside relational databases.

name: Apache Cassandra
description: Distributed wide-column database designed for massive scale with no single point of failure. Uses a peer-to-peer ring architecture with tunable consistency. Excellent write throughput and linear horizontal scalability.

name: ScyllaDB
description: High-performance, Cassandra-compatible database written in C++ using a shard-per-core architecture. Delivers significantly lower latency and higher throughput than Cassandra on the same hardware, with full CQL and Cassandra API compatibility.

name: InfluxDB
description: Purpose-built time-series database optimized for high-write-throughput metrics data. Offers a SQL-like query language (InfluxQL) and Flux scripting language, built-in data retention policies, and downsampling tasks.

name: TimescaleDB
description: Time-series database built as a PostgreSQL extension, providing automatic hypertable partitioning, columnar compression, and continuous aggregates while retaining full SQL and PostgreSQL compatibility.

name: Neo4j
description: Leading native graph database that stores data as nodes and relationships with properties. Uses the Cypher query language for expressive graph traversal queries. ACID-compliant with clustering support.

name: Elasticsearch
description: Distributed search and analytics engine built on Apache Lucene. Provides full-text search, structured search, and real-time analytics via a REST API. Core component of the Elastic Stack (ELK).

name: ClickHouse
description: Open-source columnar OLAP database designed for real-time analytics on large datasets. Achieves extremely high query performance on aggregations through columnar storage, vectorized execution, and data compression.

name: DuckDB
description: In-process analytical database designed for OLAP workloads. Runs embedded within applications (Python, R, Java, etc.) with no server required. Excels at querying Parquet, CSV, and JSON files directly with columnar vectorized execution.
use_cases: Data science and analytics in Python/R

name: CockroachDB
description: Cloud-native distributed SQL database that combines the familiarity of PostgreSQL with horizontal scalability and automatic geographic distribution. Provides serializable isolation and survives node and datacenter failures.

name: SurrealDB
description: Modern multi-model database combining relational, document, and graph capabilities in a single engine. Uses SurrealQL for unified querying across data models. Supports embedded and distributed deployment modes with built-in authentication and permissions.
